name: Eddie Miller
abstract: Edward Raymond Müller (June 23, 1911 – April 1, 1991) known professionally as Eddie Miller, was an American jazz musician who played tenor saxophone and clarinet.

interviews.title: Eddie Miller and Pete Fountain Interview, 1970-01-09

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: The Stardust Road: A Hoagy Carmichael Jubilee

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: He knew players by name, and he worshipped Eddie Miller, even in those days... And he talked about them.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Conrad "Connie" Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I can remember Eddie Miller talking about the Fern Dance Hall.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Miller was in Europe, playing with Alex Welch, and I had just finished the 28 straight days with the Jazz of the Swing Era and they went back to the states and Eddie Lockjaw Davis, Ben Webster, Eddie Miller and I went on this thing called Just for Tenor

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Bourgeois
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Miller and I started together... So we started playing together... Then I got in with a little band with Eddie Miller... Eddie Miller and I played with the Owls.

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: That was the first time I'd ever played with Eddie. ... I played with him with the Owls Orchestra, you know. ... That's when Eddie Miller was playing with them.

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: I worked there with a little three-piece group with Chink Morton's son... and Eddie Miller, who played alto sax in those days.
